build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors.

requirements.description: The player draws transit lines by clicking stations in sequence, connecting them with colored routes that trains automatically travel along. Score 0 if no line-drawing mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if lines can be drawn but no vehicles travel them. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Passengers appear at stations with shape indicators showing their destination type, automatically board trains, and disembark at matching stations. Score 0 if no passenger system exists. Score 0.5 at most if passengers appear but do not travel or have no destinations. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Stations that accumulate too many waiting passengers flash overcrowding warnings and eventually overflow, ending the game. Score 0 if stations have no capacity limit. Score 0.5 at most if warnings appear but overflow has no game-ending consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The player has limited resources (lines, carriages, tunnels) granted periodically, and must allocate them strategically as the city grows. Score 0 if resources are unlimited. Score 0.5 at most if resources are limited but new grants never arrive. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Lines can be rerouted at any time by the player, with trains smoothly transitioning to new paths and passengers re-routing accordingly. Score 0 if lines cannot be modified after placement. Score 0.5 at most if rerouting is possible but causes visual glitches or passenger loss. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

New stations of different shapes (circle, triangle, square, diamond, star) appear over time, each representing a different destination type. Score 0 if only one station type exists. Score 0.5 at most if stations appear but are all the same type. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 3 distinct map layouts with different geographic challenges: rivers requiring tunnels, islands, sprawling suburbs, or dense urban cores. Score 0 if only one map exists. Score 0.5 at most if maps differ only in station placement with no geographic features. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Resource grants offer meaningful choices: extra line, additional carriage for existing line, tunnel for river crossing, or interchange upgrade. Score 0 if no resource choices exist. Score 0.5 at most if resources are granted but offer no choice. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

City growth escalates over time: more stations appear, passenger volume increases, and new station types demand longer routes and transfers. Score 0 if the city is static. Score 0.5 at most if stations appear but passenger demand does not increase. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A run-end screen shows days survived, passengers delivered, network efficiency, and lines used. Score 0 if the game ends abruptly with no summary. Score 0.5 at most if only days survived is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Normal startup shows a styled title screen with the game name, minimalist city-map imagery, and map selection. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse text on a blank background. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The flow connects title to map selection to transit gameplay to resource-grant prompts to run-end without restarting the executable. Score 0 if major transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if transitions exist but break visually. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The map view clearly shows stations, lines, trains, passenger counts, and overcrowding warnings in readable non-overlapping regions. Score 0 if the player cannot determine network state. Score 0.5 at most if information is present but overlapping or unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Player actions produce visible feedback: line drawing shows the route forming, trains appear on new lines, overcrowding pulses warnings, passengers board visibly, and resource grants animate in. Score 0 if actions happen silently. Score 0.5 at most if only one feedback type exists. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Art direction is coherent across title, city map, stations, trains, lines, and menus with a consistent minimalist transit-map palette and style. Score 0 if mostly default controls or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Stations, trains, passengers, geographic features, and UI elements use real illustrated art with a cohesive transit-design aesthetic. Score 0 if primary elements are unstyled rectangles. Score 0.5 at most if art exists but is inconsistent in style. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation brings the network to life: trains glide along lines, passengers board and alight, stations pulse when crowded, new stations appear with fanfare, and lines draw smoothly. Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if only one element animates. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI panels use styled themed widgets with transit-map aesthetics, clean typography, or infographic-styled elements rather than raw Godot defaults. Score 0 if all UI is default Label/Button. Score 0.5 at most if some panels are styled but others remain raw defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
